qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded multipart/form-data text field buffering allows memory exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unbounded Formidable multipart text-field buffering in packages/cli/src/webhooks/webhook-form-data.ts, not CSP sandbox enforcement on webhook HTML responses or stored XSS.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Insecure code execution mode in Task Runner bypasses sandbox using new Function()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets Task Runner insecure-mode JavaScript execution through new Function(), not webhook response CSP sandbox handling or stored browser-side XSS.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing per-object authorization on MCP OAuth client deletion allows unauthorized deletion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets missing per-object authorization on MCP OAuth client deletion, not the advisory's CSP sandbox omission on webhook or related HTML responses.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause: incorrect CSP sandbox enforcement on webhook or related HTML responses allowing stored XSS with same-origin script execution. The qualifying findings cover multipart parser DoS, Task Runner insecure-mode code execution, and MCP OAuth client deletion authorization bypass.
evidence: GHSA-825q-w924-xhgx describes stored XSS caused by CSP sandbox protection not being applied correctly to webhook responses and related HTTP endpoints.
form_data_memory_exhaustion reports missing Formidable maxFields, maxFieldsSize, and maxFiles limits causing memory exhaustion DoS.
insecure_code_execution reports server-side JavaScript execution through Task Runner insecure mode and new Function().
mcp_oauth_client_idor reports missing per-object authorization for DELETE /rest/mcp/oauth-clients/:clientId.
The qualifying reports do not identify CSP header generation, sandbox directive handling, HTML response isolation, stored script rendering, or same-origin browser script execution.
